correct: I don't wake up at 7 o'clock.
explanation: Add <don't> before the verb to make a negative sentence: <I don't wake up>.

source: She brushes her teeth every morning.
correct: She doesn't brush her teeth every morning.
explanation: Use <doesn't> + base form for third person: <She doesn't brush>.

source: He goes to school by bus.
correct: Does he go to school by bus?
explanation: Use <Does> + subject + base form for questions: <Does he go>.

correct: Do they eat breakfast at 8 am?
explanation: Use <Do> + subject + base form for questions: <Do they eat>.

correct: I walked to the park.
explanation: Add <-ed> to regular verbs for past simple: <walked>.

source: She drinks coffee in the morning.
correct: She drank coffee in the morning.
explanation: <Drink> is irregular: <drank> in past simple.

correct: We don't watch TV after dinner.
explanation: Add <don't> before the verb: <We don't watch>.

correct: Do you like reading books?
explanation: Use <Do> + subject + base form: <Do you like>.

correct: He ate lunch at noon.
explanation: <Eat> is irregular: <ate> in past simple.

correct: He doesn't play football on Saturdays.
explanation: Use <doesn't> + base form: <He doesn't play>.

source: They go to bed at 10 pm.
correct: They went to bed at 10 pm.
explanation: <Go> is irregular: <went> in past simple.

source: She takes a shower every day.
correct: Does she take a shower every day?
explanation: Use <Does> + subject + base form: <Does she take>.

correct: I don't have breakfast at home.
explanation: Add <don't> before the verb: <I don't have>.

correct: She got up early.
explanation: <Get> is irregular: <got> in past simple.

correct: Do we cook dinner together?
explanation: Use <Do> + subject + base form: <Do we cook>.

source: I read a book before bed.
correct: I read a book before bed.
explanation: <Read> is irregular but looks the same in past: <read> (pronounced /rɛd/).

correct: They don't do homework after school.
explanation: Add <don't> before the verb: <They don't do>.

source: He listens to music at night.
correct: Does he listen to music at night?
explanation: Use <Does> + subject + base form: <Does he listen>.

correct: We had dinner at 7 pm.
explanation: <Have> is irregular: <had> in past simple.

source: She rides her bike to work.
correct: She doesn't ride her bike to work.
explanation: Use <doesn't> + base form: <She doesn't ride>.

source: I make my bed every morning.
correct: I made my bed every morning.
explanation: <Make> is irregular: <made> in past simple.

source: They clean the house on weekends.
correct: Do they clean the house on weekends?
explanation: Use <Do> + subject + base form: <Do they clean>.

source: He takes the dog for a walk.
correct: He doesn't take the dog for a walk.
explanation: Use <doesn't> + base form: <He doesn't take>.

correct: She wrote in her diary.
explanation: <Write> is irregular: <wrote> in past simple.

correct: Do I finish work at 5 pm?
explanation: Use <Do> + subject + base form: <Do I finish>.
